---
pagetitle: NKDE Analysis | Capstone
title: NKDE Analysis using spNetwork package
---

![](../../Images/4.png){width="500"}

# Getting Started

The code chunk below uses `p_load()` function of pacman package to check if the required packages have been installed on the computer. If they are, the packages will be launched.

The packages used are:

-   sf package is used for importing, managing, and processing geospatial data.
-   tidyverse package for aspatial data wrangling.
-   spatstat package to perform 1st-order spatial point patterns analysis and derive kernel density estimation (KDE) layer.
-   tmap package is used to visualize geospatial data on maps.

```{r}
pacman::p_load(sf, tidyverse, spatstat, lubridate, tmap, spNetwork, RColorBrewer, leaflet)
```

In the code chunk below, the [cleaned data files](DataPrep.qmd) are loaded into R.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: false
# Load base map
mpsz <- st_read(dsn = "../data/geospatial", 
               layer = "MPSZ-2019") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3414) %>%
  filter(!PLN_AREA_N %in% c("WESTERN ISLANDS", "NORTH-EASTERN ISLANDS"))

# Save the output as rds file instead of rendering the above code chunks
write_rds(mpsz, "../data/rds/mpsz.rds")
```

```{r}
# Load rds file
mpsz <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/mpsz.rds"))
pickup_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/pickup_sf.rds"))
dropoff_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/dropoff_sf.rds"))
```

The code chunk below uses `st_read()` function of sf package to import the OpenStreetMap \> Asia \> Malaysia, Singapore and Brunei shapefile extract from the [GeoFabrik website](https://download.geofabrik.de/). As this set of geospatial data is in WGS-84 coordinate system, `st_transform()` function is used to convert it to the svy21 projected coordinate system (`crs = 3414`) that Singapore uses.

```{r}
#| eval: false
roads <- st_read(dsn = "../data/geospatial", 
               layer = "gis_osm_roads_free_1") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3414) %>%
  st_sf()

roads_sg <- st_cast(mpsz) %>%
  st_intersection(roads)

str(roads_sg)
```

Since the geometry is "sfc_GEOMETRY" rather than "sfc_LINESTRING", a check is performed to ensure that the geometry of all observations are linestrings. As it appears that there are some multilinestrings, they are cast into multipoints then merged back to form a single linestring. These lines are then merged back into the original dataset. 

```{r}
#| eval: false
roads_sg_check <- roads_sg %>%
  mutate(multi = st_geometry_type(geometry)) %>%
  filter(multi != "LINESTRING")

# Cast multilinestrings into linestrings
roads_sg_check_merged <- roads_sg_check %>%
  st_cast('MULTIPOINT') %>%
  st_cast('LINESTRING')

# Merge back with linestrings
roads_sg_lines <- roads_sg %>%
  mutate(multi = st_geometry_type(geometry)) %>%
  filter(multi == "LINESTRING") %>%
  rbind(roads_sg_check_merged)

roads_sf <- st_cast(roads_sg_lines, "LINESTRING")

str(roads_sf)
```

The structure now shows "sfc_LINESTRING".

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: false
# Save the output as rds file instead of rendering the above code chunks
write_rds(roads_sf, "../data/rds/sg_road.rds")
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
# Load rds file instead of rendering the above code chunks
sg_road <- read_rds("../data/rds/sg_road.rds")
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
# Generate filtered data set
filter_data <- function(location) {
  sg_road_filtered <- sg_road %>%
    filter(PLN_AREA_N == location) %>%
    select(osm_id, name, geometry)
  
  mpsz_filtered <- mpsz %>%
    filter(PLN_AREA_N == location)
  
  pickup_filtered <- pickup_sf %>%
    st_intersection(mpsz_filtered)
  
  list(sg_road_filtered = sg_road_filtered, 
       mpsz_filtered = mpsz_filtered, 
       pickup_filtered = pickup_filtered)
}

filtered_objects <- filter_data("DOWNTOWN CORE")

# Access the filtered objects
sg_road_filtered <- filtered_objects$sg_road_filtered
mpsz_filtered <- filtered_objects$mpsz_filtered
pickup_filtered <- filtered_objects$pickup_filtered
rm(filtered_objects)
```

# Comparison of fixed bandwidths for the three methods

```{r}
#| eval: false
bw_cv_simple <- bw_cv_likelihood_calc(
  bws = seq(50,1000,50),
  lines = sg_road_filtered, 
  events = pickup_filtered,
  w = rep(1,nrow(pickup_filtered)),
  kernel_name = "quartic", method = "discontinuous",
  max_depth = 8,
  digits=2, tol=0.1, agg=5,
  sparse=TRUE, grid_shape=c(1,1),
  verbose=FALSE, check=TRUE)

# Function to get the corresponding bw value for the lowest cv_score in each column
get_max_bw <- function(cv_values) {
  sapply(cv_values[-1], function(x) cv_values$bw[which.max(x)])
}

# Get the corresponding bw values for the lowest cv_scores
max_bw_values <- get_max_bw(bw_cv_simple)

# Print the results
print(max_bw_values)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
# Calculate lixels
lixel_length <- max_bw_values/5
lixels <- lixelize_lines(sg_road_filtered, lixel_length, mindist = 50)
samples <- lines_center(lixels)
  
# Apply NKDE
densities <- nkde(sg_road_filtered, 
                  events = pickup_filtered,
                  w = rep(1, nrow(pickup_filtered)),
                  samples = samples,
                  kernel_name = "quartic",
                  bw = max_bw_values, div = "bw", 
                  method = "discontinuous", digits = 1, tol = 1,
                  grid_shape = c(1,1), max_depth = 8,
                  agg = 5, # Aggregate events within a 5m radius (faster calculation)
                  sparse = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)
  
# Rescaling to help the mapping
samples$density <- densities
lixels$density <- densities
samples$density <- samples$density*1000
lixels$density <- lixels$density*1000

# Transform lixels to WGS84
lixels <- st_transform(lixels, crs = 4326)

# Create a color palette based on the density column using kmeans
n <- 5
kmeans_bins <- kmeans(lixels$density, centers = n)$centers
color_pal <- colorNumeric(palette = c("lightblue", "lemonchiffon", "lightpink", "indianred", "firebrick"), 
                          domain = lixels$density)

# Create the leaflet map
leaflet(data = lixels) %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  addPolylines(color = ~color_pal(density), weight = 2, label = ~name) %>%
  setView(lng = mean(st_coordinates(lixels)[, 1]), lat = mean(st_coordinates(lixels)[, 2]), zoom = 14) %>%
  addLegend("bottomright", pal = color_pal, values = ~density, title = "Density", labFormat = labelFormat())
```
